description_long: This is a long, rectangular outdoor bench with a seat made of several parallel wooden planks, each separated by narrow gaps. The wood has a rich, dark brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a natural and sturdy appearance. The bench is supported by two robust, T-shaped metal legs, each with a wide, flat base for stability, likely made of concrete or painted steel. The legs are positioned at either end of the bench, leaving the middle section open and unobstructed. There are no backrests or armrests, making the bench accessible from either side and suitable for communal or public seating. The overall design is minimalist and functional, ideal for parks, sidewalks, or other urban outdoor environments.
description: A long, backless wooden bench with dark planks and sturdy T-shaped metal legs, designed for outdoor public use. The minimalist design features no armrests or backrest, emphasizing accessibility and durability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the bench, highlighting the dark wooden planks and both T-shaped metal legs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow width, the parallel arrangement of the wooden planks, and the profile of one T-shaped leg.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, showing the length and both supporting legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow width, plank arrangement, and the other T-shaped leg.
attribute_leg_design: two T-shaped metal or concrete pedestal legs
